verdict_scope: Accept the revision-explicit parity-harness repair and SST duplicate-free sampler repair for commit. This does not authorize B2 and does not replace the pending real 2372-step CUDA parity or fresh arming review.

acceptance.name: Revision-explicit parity harness
evidence: scripts/racketsport/train_ball_stage2.py:2217-2259 requires a nonblank revision and explicit expected SHA-256, resolves the revision to a commit, and loads git show <commit>:scripts/racketsport/train_ball_stage2.py rather than the working tree.
scripts/racketsport/train_ball_stage2.py:2262-2286 has no HEAD/hash inference and rejects equal baseline/candidate source hashes.
scripts/racketsport/train_ball_stage2.py:2563-2571 performs the distinct-source check before CUDA, dataset construction, or output-directory creation; output is created only at lines 2714-2717.
Fresh owned-module run passed all 31 tests. The self-comparison test reproduced return code 2, the candidate-versus-itself error, and no output directory.

An independent direct reproduction used identical simulated running-file and HEAD-blob SHA 9d2d3261... and again observed rc=2 with no artifact. Missing-revision, missing-hash, and hash-mismatch cases also failed closed.

The seven-step CPU fixture passed twice with byte-identical result values: losses [0.34526681900024414, 0.5392073392868042, 0.343158483505249, 0.5391553044319153, 0.34319964051246643, 0.5385992527008057, 0.3436070680618286] for both arms and model-state SHA 0f735e48513d0b6f270830d897dc7af35f8e0a19dc1c3d59e47a69af97262a7e for both arms.

seven_step_trace.real_compute: run_revision_explicit_compute_parity calls the baseline blob's and candidate's real train_one_stage2_batch; that executes forward, BCE-with-logits, weighted reduction, backward, and optimizer.step, then saves and reloads actual checkpoints
seven_step_trace.limitation: Tiny synthetic CPU compute proof, not production WASB/B0 decoding and not the 2372-step CUDA gate.

acceptance.name: SST within-batch uniqueness via Option B
evidence: Historical sampler reproduction at sample_count=1001, batch_size=8, seed=20260722 produced step 1751 [256,432,546,743,401,497,432,947], 7 unique.
Candidate produced step 1751 [256,432,546,743,401,497,120,947], 8 unique, and moved 432 into the next batch in place of 120; this is an in-permutation cross-boundary swap, not a drop or redraw.

scripts/racketsport/train_ball_stage2.py:3778-3837 uses one private seeded randperm per permutation, swaps only colliding splice-prefix elements, preserves the complete permutation multiset, and asserts dataset-index uniqueness.
scripts/racketsport/train_ball_stage2.py:3872-3894 installs the distinct sampler only in the SST loader; lines 2863-2869 enter it only in dual-SST mode.
scripts/racketsport/train_ball_stage2.py:3917-3933 independently rejects missing or duplicate semantic sample IDs; lines 2947-2950 run that guard before pseudo forward/backward.
Fresh owned-module tests passed the exact reviewer reproduction, boundary splice, deterministic restart/resume, and semantic duplicate-ID refusal.

reasoning: The repair changes temporal pairing at a splice by delaying a colliding repeat and advancing the first later nonblocked row. That local anti-repeat correlation is intentional and is a real distributional change from the defective stream.

The algorithm is label-equivariant: it compares sample identities only for equality and never orders or thresholds numeric indices. The first later nonblocked row in a uniform permutation is uniform over eligible rows, so manifest/index order is not favored.

Every generated permutation retains every row exactly once. At any finite batch prefix, exposure is therefore exactly floor(total_rows_drawn/N) or ceil(total_rows_drawn/N); the six-case sweep confirmed the exact quotient/remainder histogram.

An additional independent high-collision n=11 probe over 220000 batches found exact 160000 exposures per ID, pair-count coefficient of variation 0.000846, and per-ID mean interarrival approximately 11.0.
distribution_review.random_draws: Historical and candidate production-shape probes each made 19 randperm calls for 2372 batches, had identical raw randperm streams/private-generator final state, and left global CPU RNG unchanged.

acceptance.name: Human-only path invariance
evidence: AST audit against pre-repair HEAD 9d2d3261... found all 26 imports unchanged and no module global/constant added, removed, or changed.

DeterministicFullBatchSampler, _make_training_loader, _assert_human_training_batch, train_one_stage2_batch, apply_occlusion_augmentation, CombinedStage2Dataset/B0 dataset code, _no_cache_cycle, save/load checkpoint compute, and imported train_ball_pretrain dependency are unchanged.

The run() diff changes only the dual_sst_mode loader call. The no-SST branch at lines 2934-2945 still invokes the historical human-only training step with the same loader, seed, optimizer, augmentation parameters, and step count.
_training_data_summary returns human_dataset.summary before the changed SST-only description branch.

The distinct-baseline fixture is arm-A-equivalent: no SST manifest, dataset, loader, assertion, forward, or loss is involved, and exact losses, sample order, RNG consumption, final state, and loaded checkpoint state matched.
acceptance.non_compute_difference: The two new parser selectors serialize as null fields in ordinary candidate checkpoint args. Raw checkpoint bytes/metadata therefore differ even though human compute and model state are identical.

acceptance.name: Wide-suite reporting and regression attribution
evidence: The repair's exact wide run recorded 4414 passed, 42 failed, 25 skipped, exit 1, and zero failures in the owned trainer/test module. It did not claim a green suite or prove all failures at clean HEAD.
Against ball_audio_anchor_20260722/wide_suite_failures.txt: repair 42, earlier classified 41, intersection 39, repair-only 3, earlier-only 2.
Earlier-only nodes are the obsolete parity-premise test, now intentionally renamed/repaired and passing, plus an event-dataset node that now passes.

Repair-only nodes are one flight-simulator timing threshold and two Track-C player-selection/pooling nodes. A fresh review rerun produced flight 12.048 seconds versus the 5-second threshold and both Track-C nodes passed; no trainer path was entered.

The durable coordinator addendum at runs/lanes/trackE_datahygiene_20260722/RESUME_BRIEF.md:26 identifies best_stack rev-15 drift as KNOWN_ATTRIBUTED(trkC_constraints_wire). Track C's own spec owns best_stack, process_video, player-selection code/tests, and the revision bump.

The otherwise unresolved ball-arc and event-eval nodes are both present in the earlier 41-failure run, their files are tracked-clean relative to this repair, and neither imports or reaches train_ball_stage2. No residual failure is attributable to this repair.
acceptance.wide_suite_rerun_reason: The dispatch made the wide rerun optional under documented concurrent drift; the review instead performed exact failure-set reconciliation and focused residual reruns.

acceptance.name: New candidate identity
evidence: Fresh shasum -a 256 of scripts/racketsport/train_ball_stage2.py is b8e653dab6d906070bfeabc146035f00b3adf8b632e1c12f1c5f1e043898bbc7.
The future CUDA run and fresh arming review must use this full SHA-256, not obsolete candidate 9d2d3261....

classification: configs/racketsport/best_stack.json rev 15 and player-selection/process_video files belong to trkC_constraints_wire_20260722.
Court keypoint/data files belong to concurrent court lanes.
Audio/event files belong to concurrent Track-B/Track-D lanes.
No repair transcript patch target was outside the trainer, its owned test, or repair lane directory.

review_r4_minimum_reopen_conditions.condition: Repair and independently ultra-review revision/hash-pinned parity plus SST sample-ID uniqueness.
B1 gate PASS with completed decoding, accepted_windows>=1000, accepted_sources>=5, zero holdout rows, decodable windows, agreement reasons, exact hashes, and trainer replay validation.
Real 2372-step CUDA parity, no CPU fallback, all losses/sample arrays/final state/checkpoint/environment/hashes.
Builder pin finalized exactly as review_r4 specifies.
Resume lane ultra adversarial review ACCEPT, including telemetry/whole-manifest-byte-identity resolution.
Fresh arming review supersedes review_r4 DO_NOT_ARM after all prior evidence exists.

explicitly_excluded_from_commit_scope: all concurrent Track-C, court, audio, event, data-ledger, and manager-file drift

tests_run_by_review.command: Independent DeterministicDistinctFullBatchSampler sweep: 226000 batches each at N=1000,1001,1007,1023,2003,2049; batch_size=8; seed=20260722
PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 ... pytest flight timing + Track-C player-selection + pooling-wire residual nodes

honest_issues: The repository-wide suite is red: 4414 passed, 42 failed, 25 skipped, and the failures were not all proven at clean HEAD. ACCEPT is scoped to this repair because exact set reconciliation and dependency-focused reruns found no trainer regression.
The CPU parity fixture is seven tiny synthetic steps. It does not run production WASB, real B0 decoding, CUDA, or the required 2372 steps.
B1, the builder pin, the resume ultra review, real CUDA parity, and a fresh explicit arming review remain open. B2 remains DO_NOT_ARM and VERIFIED=0.
Candidate parser additions alter checkpoint args metadata/raw checkpoint bytes even on human-only runs, although compute/sample order/model state are identical.

Same-candidate sampler resume offsets are exact, but pre-repair dual-SST checkpoints do not pin trainer/sampler source and must not be treated as cross-version schedule-compatible. B2's fresh commands forbid resume; the broader resume review remains open.

The repair's schema-constrained report distributes the requested option/hash/invariance/classification details across its acceptance rows and dedicated proof JSONs rather than exposing every requested item as a top-level report key. The evidence is internally consistent, but consumers should read the proof artifacts with report.json.
